best_response.text:
A. FROZEN: RIGHT UPPER LOBE WEDGE: (A91, TP, smear): Poorly differentiated squamous cell carcinoma.

NOTE
The size of the tumor of 3.8 cm reflects the tumor present in both wedge biopsy and lobectomy. Special studies show that tumor cells are positive for P63 and CK 5, negative for TTF-1, synaptophysin, and chromogranin, supporting above diagnosis. Immunohistochemistry controls are examined and show appropriate reactivity.
Lung Cancer Summary
Specimen type: Lung
Procedure: Wedge biopsy and lobectomy
Specimen integrity: Intact
Tumor location: Right upper lobe
Tumor focality: Unifocal
Tumor size: 3.8 cm
Histologic type: Squamous cell carcinoma, basaloid type
Histologic grade: Grade 3
Treatment effect: N/A
Status of margins:
    Bronchial: Free of tumor
    Vascular: Free of tumor
    Parenchymal: Free of tumor
    Pleural: Free of tumor
Visceral/Parietal pleural invasion: Absent
Lymphatic vascular invasion: Present
Lymph node status: Positive for metastatic tumor at level 12R, negative at level 2R, 4R, and 8
Extension into other structures: Absent
Non-neoplastic pathology: N/A
TMN stage: pT2AN1PL0
GROSS DESCRIPTION
A. The specimen is labeled right upper lobe wedge. Received fresh for frozen section, is a 9 x 6.1 x 2.2 cm lung wedge with a 9cm staple line. The specimen is previously opened and reveals a 2.5 x 2.2 x 1.8 cm white-tan, irregular, firm tumor. The tumor is 0.1 cm to the nearest margin underneath the staple line. The staple line is removed and the exposed parenchyma is inked black. Representative sections of the tumor are submitted for frozen section with the remnant put in cassette A91. A touch prep is performed; more cassettes are submitted as follows: A1 through A3 sections of tumor with the nearest margin, A4 lung parenchyma underlying the staple line, A5 unaffected lung.
B. This specimen is labeled level XII R. lymph node. The specimen is received fresh and consists of multiple anthracotic lymph node fragments

best_response.text:
measuring in aggregate 1.1 x 0.9 x 0.2 cm. The specimen is entirely submitted
in cassette B1.
C. The specimen is labeled right upper lobe. Received without fixative is a
145 g, 17.0 x 7.0 x 2.7 cm lobe of lung with stapled bronchial and vascular
margins. There is a 13.5 cm in length stapled line adjacent to the hilum and
a 14.0 cm in length stapled line on the opposite side. A palpable mass is
felt and the overlying pleura is inked green. The staple line adjacent to the
pleura is removed and the exposed parenchyma is inked yellow. The staple
line on the opposite side is removed and the exposed parenchyma is inked
black. Sectioning shows a 1.3 x 1.3 x 1.2 cm firm tan mass that grossly abuts
the anti-hilar staple line (black) and is 0.2 cm subjacent to the overlying
pleura (green), is 1.0 cm from the hilar staple line (yellow) and is 1.5 cm
from the bronchial margin. Additionally, there is a 1.0 x 0.9 x 0.7 cm
poorly-defined hemorrhagic subpleural nodule located greater than 5.0 cm from
the bronchial margin and greater than 2.0 cm from both staple lines. The
remainder of the specimen consists of spongy red lung parenchyma without
nodules or other lesions.
C1 - bronchial margin, C2 - vascular margins, C3 and C4 - mass with overlying
anti-hilar staple margin (black) and pleural (green), C5 - additional sections
of nearest pleural and anti-hilar staple margins, C6 - hilar staple margin
(yellow) closest to firm nodule, C7 - hemorrhagic nodule, C8 - representative
sections of unremarkable lung.
D. The specimen is labeled 4R lymph node. The specimen is received fresh and
consists of multiple tan-pink and anthracotic lymph node fragments measuring
in aggregate 1.1 x 0.9 x 0.2 cm. The specimen is entirely submitted in
cassette D1.
E. This specimen is labeled 2R lymph node. The specimen is received fresh
and consists of a single portion of anthracotic soft tissue that measures 0.3
x 0.2 x 0.2 cm. The specimen is entirely submitted in cassette E1.
F. This specimen is labeled 8 lymph node. The specimen is received fresh and
consists of a single anthracotic portion of lymph node that measures 0.4 x
0.4 x 0.2 cm. The specimen is entirely submitted in cassette F1
SPECIMEN(S) RECEIVED
A: FROZEN:RIGHT UPPER lOBE WEDGE
B: LEVEL 12R LYMPH NODE
C: FRESH-RIGHT UPPER LOBE
D: FRESH- 4R LYMPH NODE
E: FRESH -2R LYMPH NODE
F: FRESH-8 LYMPH NODE
* The above listed tests (if any) were developed and the performance
characteristics determined by epartment of Pathology. These tests have
not been cleared or approved for commercial use by the U.S. Food and Drug
Administration. The FDA has determined that FDA review is not required for
such in-house assays. (21 CFR 809.30(e))
OPERATIVE INFORMATION
DIAGNOSIS: LUNG MASS; PROCEDURE: BRONCHOSCOPY FLEXIBLE FIBEROPTIC,

Completed By (Interviewer Name on OpenClinica) _____________________________
